title: Wano Prepares While the World Shakes
description: On pages 3–6, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, Momonosuke, World News Voices, Unknown person #2, Kozuki Alliance, Vivi as The raid preparations accelerate while outside Wano, Reverie news shakes the world.
scene: The raid preparations accelerate while outside Wano, Reverie news shakes the world.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Fishman Island Ryugu kingdom Ryugu Castle

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and the state of affairs on the other side of the world are...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, that brings an end to our long journey!

dialogue.speaker: Momonosuke
dialogue.text: Thank you so much, Luffy's grandfather!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You're probably pretty tired, Neptune. BUwahaha!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'm exhausted.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Garp, you should also put your feet up for a little before leaving.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, I wouldn't say no to a little tea, but with things the way they are...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I don't think I could stay for much longer!

dialogue.speaker: World News Voices
dialogue.text: Reverie always stirs up a ruckus.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's no way that 50-plus countries could ever gather in one place and all agree to just "continue get along."

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Each has their own problems at home.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The strong countries are constantly staring each other down,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And they set their subordinates at each other's throats. Resources and technology are but tools in these fights.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Differences in wealth and religion

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Preciude real egali- tarianism.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even if they do manage to join hands, these countries'll smile while stepping on each other's feet.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How many kings are actually willing to speak for their people, or for less fortunate nations?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: These round tables are in a sorry state.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I agree that conversations among kings are rather touchy.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and history between nations means the roots of grudges to run deep.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, as long as no blood is spilled,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I say we have peace.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: But this year... was bad.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I apologize for telling you all so late, but... the truth is that something happened that you have not yet been made aware of.

dialogue.speaker: World News Voices
dialogue.text: After Reverie was adjourned,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The incident occurred. Immediately after our ship had set off.

dialogue.speaker: Kozuki Alliance
dialogue.text: I received the report while aboard.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Marines are currently sparing no effort to see the situation resolved.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I just ask that you... not let this color your opinion of us humans aboveground.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: The incident involves the kingdom of Alabasta.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: No...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Miss Vivi?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The present day one week has passed since Reverie's adjournment.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Amidst the settling tumult of this year's happenings,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: The REPRE- Sentatives of each country

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Make their separate ways home.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Let us turn back the clock a little.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: We news, Inc.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: And no more! Then he

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: It's incident after incident!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: PRRR!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How in the world can so much stuff happen at once?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Get me a Kickin' title for this!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: President! Which of these stories are we going to put on the front page?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: PRRR!

summary: This section advances the Wano - Before the Raid and World News storyline with 46 dialogue beats. The raid preparations accelerate while outside Wano, Reverie news shakes the world.
